content: The Nyquist plot of a strictly stable $G(s)$ having the numerator polynomial as $(s-3)$ encircles the critical point -1 once in the anti-clockwise direction. Which one of the following statements on the closed-loop system shown in figure, is correct?

options.content: The system stability cannot be ascertained.

explanation: $$ \begin{aligned} &\begin{array}{ll} P=0, & N=+1 \\ N \neq P & \end{array}\\ &\therefore \text { Closed loop system is unstable. } \end{aligned} $$

content:
In the Nyquist plot of the open-loop transfer function

$$G(s)H(s) = {{3s + 5} \over {s - 1}}$$

corresponding to the feedback loop shown in the figure, the infinite semi-circular arc of the Nyquist contour in s-plane is mapped into a point at
explanation:
Given open loop transfer function, $G(s) H(s)=\frac{3 s+5}{s-1}$

$$ \Rightarrow G(s) H(s)=\frac{s\left(3+\frac{5}{s}\right)}{s\left(1-\frac{1}{s}\right)}=\frac{3+\frac{5}{s}}{1-\frac{1}{s}} $$

The infinite semicircular arc of the Nyquist contour in s-plane is mapped into a point at.

$$ \left.\Rightarrow G(s) H(s)\right|_{s=\infty}=\frac{3+0}{1-0}=3 $$

Hence, the correct option is (C).

explanation:

content: The Bode magnitude plot for the transfer function $\frac{V_0(s)}{V_i(s)}$ of the circuit is as shown. The value of $R$ is $\Omega$. (Round off to 2 decimal places)
explanation:
Given circuit is as shown below,

Transforming the above circuit in Laplace domain

Here, $L=1 \mathrm{mH}, C=250 \mu \mathrm{~F}$

The transfer function $T(s)$ for above circuit is given by,

$$ \begin{aligned} & T(s)=\frac{V_0(s)}{V_i(s)}=\frac{\frac{1}{s C}}{R+s L+\frac{1}{s C}} \\ & T(s)=\frac{V_0(s)}{V_i(s)}=\frac{\frac{1}{s C}}{\frac{s^2 L C+s C R+1}{s C}} \\ & T(s)=\frac{V_0(s)}{V_i(s)}=\frac{\frac{1}{L C}}{s^2+\frac{R}{L} s+\frac{1}{L C}} \end{aligned} $$

So, characteristic equation of above transfer function is,

$$ s^2+\frac{R}{L} s+\frac{1}{L C}=0 \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(i)$$

Comparing equation (i) with standard characteristics equation, $s^2+2 \xi \omega_n s+\omega_n^2=0$ then

$$ \begin{aligned} \therefore \quad \omega_n & =\frac{1}{\sqrt{L C}} \\ \xi & =\frac{R}{2} \sqrt{\frac{C}{L}} \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(ii)\end{aligned} $$

Now from the Bode plot, resonant peak $M_r$ is 26 dB at $\omega_r=2000 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{sec}$,

$$ \begin{aligned} & \left(M_r\right)_{\mathrm{dB}}=26 \mathrm{~dB}=20 \log M_r \\ & M_r=19.95 \approx 20 \end{aligned} $$

So,

$$ M_r=\frac{1}{2 \xi \sqrt{1-\xi^2}}=20 $$

Solving above equation, we get $\xi=0.025$

Now from equation (ii),

$$ \begin{aligned} & \xi=\frac{R}{2} \sqrt{\frac{C}{L}} \\ & 0.025=\frac{R}{2} \sqrt{\frac{250 \times 10^{-6}}{1 \times 10^{-3}}} \\ & 0.000625=\frac{R^2}{4} \times 0.25 \\ & R^2=0.01 \Omega \\ & R=0.1 \Omega \end{aligned} $$

Hence, the correct answer is 0.1 .

explanation:

Let $T(s)=K e^{-s T}$

Where, $T \rightarrow$ Transportation lag.

From bode plot, $20 \log K=8 \mathrm{~dB}$

$$ K=2.511 $$

At $\omega=1 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{sec}, \phi=-60^{\circ}$

From transfer function, $\phi=-\omega T \times \frac{180^{\circ}}{\pi}$

$$ -60^{\circ}=-T \times \frac{180^{\circ}}{\pi} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} & T=1.047 \\\\ & T(s)=2.511 e^{-1.047 s} \end{aligned} $$

Hence, the correct option is (D).

content:
Consider a lead compensator of the form

$$K(s) = {{1 + {s \over a}} \over {1 + {s \over {\beta a}}}},\beta > 1,a > 0$$

The frequency at which this compensator produces maximum phase lead is 4 rad/s. At this frequency, the gain amplification provided by the controller, assuming asymptotic Bode-magnitude plot of $$K(s)$$, is 6 dB. The values of $$a,\beta$$, respectively, are
explanation:
Given the form of the lead compensator:

$ K(s) = \frac{1 + \frac{s}{a}}{1 + \frac{s}{\beta a}},\ \beta > 1,\ a > 0 $

At the frequency ω = 4 rad/s, this compensator produces the maximum phase lead, and the gain amplification provided by the controller, according to the asymptotic Bode-magnitude plot of $ K(s) $, is 6 dB. We need to find the values of $ a $ and $ \beta $.

Rewriting the given compensator:

$ K(s)=\frac{1 + \frac{s}{\alpha}}{1 + \frac{s}{\alpha \beta}} $

$ K(s)=\left(\frac{s + \alpha}{\alpha}\right) \times \left(\frac{\alpha \beta}{s + \alpha \beta}\right) $

$ K(s)=\frac{\beta(s + \alpha)}{s + \alpha \beta} $

$ \sqrt{\alpha \times \alpha \beta} = 4 $

This results in:

$ \alpha \sqrt{\beta} = 4 \ \text{..........(i)} $

Also:

$ \alpha \beta > \alpha \ \text{...........(ii)} $

Considering the magnitude of $ K(j \omega) $:

$ K(j \omega) = \frac{\beta(j \omega + \alpha)}{j \omega + \alpha \beta} $

$ |K(j \omega)| = \frac{\beta \sqrt{\omega^2 + \alpha^2}}{\sqrt{\omega^2 + (\alpha \beta)^2}} $

Given that at 4 rad/sec, the gain is 6 dB:

$ 20 \log_{10}|K(j \omega)| = 6 \ \text{dB} $

$ \Rightarrow |K(j \omega)| = 2 $

$ \Rightarrow \frac{\beta \sqrt{16 + \alpha^2}}{\sqrt{16 + (\alpha \beta)^2}} = 2 $

$ \Rightarrow \frac{\beta^2(16 + \alpha^2)}{16 + (\alpha \beta)^2} = 4 $

$ \Rightarrow \beta^2(16 + \alpha^2) = 64 + 4(\alpha \beta)^2 $

This simplifies to:

$ \alpha^2 \beta^2 + 16 \beta^2 = 64 + 4(\alpha \beta)^2 $

$ \Rightarrow 16 \beta^2 = 64 + 3(\alpha \beta)^2 $

By solving the above equations, it is found that only the values $ \alpha = 2 $ and $ \beta = 4 $ satisfy all the conditions (i), (ii), and (iii).

Thus, the correct values are $ a = 2 $ and $ \beta = 4 $.

answer:

(b) Phase margin $$ = - {36^0}$$
(c) Gain margin $$-14$$ $$dB$$
(d) As Gain margin and phase margin both are negative then system is unstable.

content:
Open-loop transfer function of a unity - feedback system is $$$G\left( s \right) = {G_1}\left( s \right).{e^{ - s{\tau _D}}} = {{{e^{ - s{\tau _D}}}} \over {s\left( {s + 1} \right)\left( {s + 2} \right)}}$$$
Given : $$\,\left| {{G_1}\left( {j\omega } \right)} \right| \approx 1$$ when $$\omega = 0.446$$

(a) Determine the phase margin when $${\tau _D} = 0$$
(b) Comment in one sentence on the effect of dead time on the stability of the system.
(c) Determine the maximum value of dead time $${\tau _D}$$ for the closed-loop system to be stable.
answer:
(a) $${52^ \circ }$$
(b) When dead time increases the stability of the system decreases
(c) $${\tau _D}$$ $$=1.947$$

content: The asymptotic magnitude Body plot of a system is given in Figure. Find the transfer function of the system analytically. It is known that the system is minimal phase system.
